No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to ending hunger or improving nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to health or well-being outcomes.

Quality Education.reason: The course's core purpose is to train professional Korean language educators, covering teaching theory, curriculum design, and practical experience, directly supporting quality education and teacher supply (Targets 4.1, 4.4, 4.c).
evidence: 積極培育具備專業素養之韓語教育人才 (actively cultivate professional Korean language education talents)
韓文教材教法 (Teaching Methods and Materials of Korean)
為未來從事韓語教育奠定堅實基礎 (lay a solid foundation for future careers in Korean language education)

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to energy access or renewable energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly aims to prepare students for 'future employment or further studies' in Korean language education, directly contributing to productive employment and decent work (Target 8.5).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to industry, innovation, or infrastructure development.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: While offering diverse language options is positive, the course does not explicitly address reducing inequalities based on age, sex, disability, or other statuses as defined by SDG 10 targets.
evidence: 除英語、日語等主流外語課程外，亦有必要開設韓語相關課程供學生選修 (Besides mainstream foreign language courses such as English and Japanese, it is also necessary to offer Korean-related courses)

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to sustainable urbanization or community development.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to sustainable consumption or production patterns.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to climate change mitigation or adaptation.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems or ocean conservation.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to peace, justice, or institutional strength.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course involves 'educational internships in high schools' and 'collaborative compilation of teaching materials,' demonstrating partnerships and capacity building for educational goals (Targets 17.9, 17.17).
